Nessa Calloway (2025)
Bryce Emmet. He held Stag from 2022 to 2024 and passed it to Calloway for 2025.
Orla Venn (2025)
Kester Mace, who held the Owl banner from 2020 to 2024
No. Venn is the current Owl and has no championship record. The Owl's 2023 championship was Mace's, not Venn's.
He chose to pass Owl to Orla Venn and created a new banner, Raven.
Kingfisher (created new for 2025 after giving up Stag)
Owl (Orla Venn) and Kingfisher (Bryce Emmet)
The original scorecard listed Heron (566) at rank 3 and Raven (567) at rank 4, but Raven's total is higher than Heron's. The ranks should be swapped.
Rank 3 (corrected from original rank 4)
No. Darke protested but the Marshal confirmed the correction was arithmetic, not a judgment call. Darke withdrew the protest.
Bryce Emmet (Kingfisher, eliminated)
He was registered as a competitor (eliminated) and served as assistant range officer. He did not compete on Day 2.
Orla Venn, substituting for Ingrid Paal who had a scheduling conflict
No. She was eliminated on Day 1 and served as substitute scorer on Day 2.
Raven (Kester Mace). Fox won 104–98.
Heron (Sefton Darke). Stag won 101–94.
It was released during a wind gust and struck the target boss frame, scoring zero.
That it was a valid shot scoring zero, not a misfire. The shot stands as scored.
Because the gust arrived during the release, not before, so he could not call a hold in time.
No. The protest was noted but denied.
Two: one against the qualifying rank correction, one against the wind-affected arrow ruling. Both were denied.
Fox 108, Stag 106
Substitute scorer Orla Venn
1st Fox (597), 2nd Stag (585), 3rd Raven (567), 4th Heron (566)
Kester Mace, competing under the Owl banner
No. The 2020 Owl was Kester Mace. The 2025 Owl is Orla Venn. They are different people.
Ingrid Paal (2019–2024)
Yes, from 2021 through 2025 (retained own banner)
Assistant range officer (not as a competitor — he was eliminated)
No conflict-of-interest objections were filed. The Marshal's closing notes state Emmet and Venn served without conflict-of-interest objections.
No. No safety incidents were reported. The wind-affected arrow was ruled a valid shot, not a safety incident.
Bryce Emmet. He held the Stag in 2022 when the championship was won. Calloway is the 2025 Stag holder but the 2022 championship belongs to Emmet.
Bryce Emmet (competitor as Kingfisher AND assistant range officer on Day 2) and Orla Venn (competitor as Owl AND substitute scorer on Day 2)
